description: Fix silent data loss in export-har buffer clearing — merge entries from clearing fetches instead of discarding them

userStories.title: Merge entries from clearing dispatches in export-har to prevent data loss
userStories.description: As a developer using network capture, I want HAR exports to include all captured requests so that requests arriving between the initial fetch and the clear are not silently dropped
acceptanceCriteria: When args.clear is true, the response data from the clearing getNetworkRequests dispatch is merged into the entries array (not discarded)
When args.clear is true and includeWebSocketFrames is true, the response data from the clearing getWebSocketFrames dispatch is also merged into the entries array
Duplicate entries (same request appearing in both the initial fetch and the clear fetch) are deduplicated before merging
When args.clear is false, behavior is unchanged — only the initial fetch is performed
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/export-har.ts, lines 194-249

Current flow:
1. Fetch network requests WITHOUT clearing (line 202-207)
2. Optionally fetch WebSocket frames WITHOUT clearing (line 212-217)
3. If args.clear, re-dispatch getNetworkRequests with clear:true (line 225) — response DISCARDED
4. If args.clear and includeWebSocketFrames, re-dispatch getWebSocketFrames with clear:true (line 227) — response DISCARDED

The problem: Between step 1 and step 3, new network requests may have arrived. The clear dispatch returns those new requests but the code discards the response. Those requests are silently lost.

Fix approach:
- On the clearing dispatches (lines 225-228), capture the response data
- Parse the response the same way as the initial fetch (requestsRaw handling pattern)
- Convert new entries using requestToHarEntry / wsFrameToHarEntry
- Merge into the entries array
- To deduplicate, use a Set of request identifiers. The CapturedRequest type has startTime and url fields which together form a unique key. Alternatively, use the requestId field if present.
- If deduplication is complex, a simpler approach: since the initial fetch did NOT clear, the clearing fetch's response will contain ALL entries (old + new). Just replace the initial entries with the clearing fetch's entries instead of merging. This is simpler and avoids dedup entirely.

IMPORTANT: The sort at lines 232-236 using localeCompare on ISO 8601 strings is fine — do not change it.

WARNING: Do NOT change the two-phase fetch design (fetch-without-clear then clear-separately). This design prevents data loss when the second fetch (WebSocket frames) fails. The fix is only about using the clearing fetch's response instead of discarding it.
